book: Three Books of Testimonies Against the Jews.

lineage: Three Books of Testimonies Against the Jews.
That the yoke of the law was heavy, which is cast off by us, and that the Lord's yoke is easy, which is taken up by us.

title: That the yoke of the law was heavy, which is cast off by us, and that the Lord's yoke is easy, which is taken up by us.

source.title: ANF05. Fathers of the Third Century: Hippolytus, Cyprian, Caius, Novatian, Appendix

verses.book: Three Books of Testimonies Against the Jews.
verses.text: That the yoke of the law was heavy, which is cast off by us, and that the Lord’s yoke is easy, which is taken up by us.

verses.book: Three Books of Testimonies Against the Jews.
verses.text: In the second Psalm: “Wherefore have the heathen been in tumult, and the peoples meditated vain things?

verses.book: Three Books of Testimonies Against the Jews.
verses.text: The kings of the earth have stood up, and their princes have been gathered together against the Lord, and against His Christ.

verses.book: Three Books of Testimonies Against the Jews.
verses.text: Let us break their bonds asunder, and cast away from us their yoke.”

verses.book: Three Books of Testimonies Against the Jews.
verses.text: Also in the Gospel according to Matthew: “Come unto me, ye who labour and are burdened, and I will make you to rest.

verses.book: Three Books of Testimonies Against the Jews.
verses.text: Take my yoke upon you, and learn of me: for I am meek and lowly of heart, and ye shall find rest for your souls.

verses.book: Three Books of Testimonies Against the Jews.
verses.text: For my yoke is good, and my burden is light.”

verses.book: Three Books of Testimonies Against the Jews.
verses.text: Also in the Acts of the Apostles: “It seemed good to the Holy Ghost, and to us, to impose upon you no other burden than those things which are of necessity, that you should abstain from idolatries, from shedding of blood, and from fornication.

verses.book: Three Books of Testimonies Against the Jews.
verses.text: And whatsoever you would not to be done unto you, do not to others.”
